div.head: Memorandum by Mr. Lauchlin Currie, Administrative Assistant to President Roosevelt, to the Chief of the Division of Far Eastern Affairs (Hamilton)
div.p: You will be interested in the attached excerpt from a letter from John Fairbank,Representative in China of the Interdepartmental Committee for the Acquisition of Foreign Publications (IDC), of the Library of Congress, and of the Office of Strategic Services (OSS). dated December 30. The Ta Kung Pao editorial did not come out until the fifth.

head.hi: Excerpt From a Letter From Professor John K. Fairbank to Mr. Currie, Dated December 30, 1942

frus:attachment.p: The latest bombshell here is the story, from Franklin Ho, that the British delegation at the IPEInstitute of Pacific Relations. conference in Canada brought out a proposal to give Manchuria to Russia or to internationalise it. The GimoGeneralissimo Chiang Kai-shek, President of the Chinese Executive Yuan (Premier). got this report by cable and hit the ceiling; it has not yet been published or reported in detail. It is regarded as the most serious development in a long time (and is rather a climax to the love feast indulged in by both parties to the British Parliamentary Mission here earlier this month.)
